vehicles.slot: Small round cap on top-left corner of shifter console — pry off to access slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Small round cap on top-left corner of shifter console — pry off to access slot.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Small round cap on top-left corner of shifter console — pry off to access slot.
vehicles.notes: Key interlock recall Service Bulletin 05-009 affects some units — verify recall was completed.
vehicles.warn: Always apply parking brake and chock wheels before releasing Park on any incline.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near the gear selector base on the center console, covered by a removable plastic cap
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DCT dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Small slot near the gear selector base on the center console, covered by a removable plastic cap.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
DCT REQUIRES POWER: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DCT transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.notes: 8-speed DCT: power strongly preferred. Flatbed tow only for DCT variants.
vehicles.warn: 8-speed DCT: FLATBED ONLY. Do not tow with wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap.
vehicles.notes: Brake lights must be functioning for the normal shift interlock to work. If brake lights are out, use the manual slot.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: Slot on front-right side of shifter base — snap-off plastic cover
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot on front-right side of shifter base — snap-off plastic cover.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot on front-right side of shifter base — snap-off plastic cover.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: FRONT LIP of center console near the dashboard — silver rectangular slot. NOT near the shift handle.
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
ZF 9HP transmission: locate the shift lock slot — FRONT LIP of center console near the dashboard — silver rectangular slot. NOT near the shift handle..
Insert a thin key or dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 from dealer tool suppliers) into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
NOTE: The ZF 9HP solenoid requires more force than most vehicles — apply steady firm pressure.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
ZF 9HP STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
The ZF 9HP is very difficult to shift without 12V power — jumping the battery first is the most reliable method.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
If using mechanical slot: a dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is more reliable than a key.
vehicles.notes: CRITICAL: The slot is at the FRONT of the console near the dashboard, NOT at the shifter base — this is a very common mistake. Always carry a jump pack with ZF 9HP-equipped vehicles.
vehicles.warn: ZF 9HP: jump pack strongly recommended. Forcing shift without power risks transmission damage.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — SH-AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Shift Lock Requires Power
knownIssues.detail: The ZF 9HP 9-speed transmission requires 12V power to shift. Without power the standard slot method is very difficult. Always carry a jump pack. A dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is the most reliable mechanical option.

knownIssues.title: Shift Lock Slot Location Misidentified
knownIssues.detail: The shift lock slot is at the FRONT lip of the center console near the dashboard — NOT near the shift handle base. Technicians and operators regularly probe the wrong location. Look at where the console meets the dash.

battery.warnings: ZF 9HP: must restore power before TC shift. Use jump pack rated for AGM batteries.

vehicles.slot: Near base of electronic gear selector on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Near base of electronic gear selector on center console.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Electronic selector design. Slot location may vary by trim — consult owner's manual.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — SH-AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap.
vehicles.notes: 2007-2009: AWD (SH-AWD) standard on all models. 2010-2012: FWD option added.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

neutral: Apply parking brake.

vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Slot on right side of shifter base, snap-off plastic cover
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot on right side of shifter base, snap-off plastic cover.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.

Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.

vehicles.notes: SH-AWD available with 5-speed auto (2009-2011), 6-speed auto (2012-2014), or 6-speed manual (2010-2014). SH-AWD: flatbed only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. SH-AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Slot at base of gear selector on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DCT dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Slot at base of gear selector on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
DCT REQUIRES POWER: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DCT transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.notes: Power method preferred for 8-speed DCT. 9-speed (2018+): slot method works reliably.
vehicles.warn: 8-speed DCT: FLATBED ONLY. Do not tow with all four wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. SH-AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

knownIssues.title: 8-Speed DCT Cannot Be Towed on Wheels
knownIssues.detail: 2015-2017 TLX with 8-speed DCT must be flatbedded. Towing with driven wheels on the ground will destroy the dual-clutch transmission. Always verify which transmission is installed before attempting wheel-lift.

battery.warnings: 8-speed DCT models: restore power before attempting shift

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Manual trans: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Manual trans: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (key to Run position — do not start).
Locate the shift lock override: small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off the plastic cap with your ignition key or a small flathead screwdriver.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base; manual has no shift lock
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: locate cap near selector base. Pry off. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch and move gear lever to Neutral.
Mid-engine vehicle — battery is in the FRONT trunk (frunk), not at rear engine.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
BATTERY IS IN THE FRONT TRUNK (frunk) — NSX is mid-engine, battery is NOT at the rear.
Open the front hood to access battery. Connect jump pack.
Once powered, shift normally.
RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
Very low clearance and high value — use flatbed with wheel grids only.
vehicles.notes: Mid-engine. Battery in frunk (front storage). High-value vehicle.
vehicles.warn: Battery in FRUNK — not at rear engine. Very high value — use padded equipment only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Flatbed strongly preferred.
towNotes: Battery in frunk. RWD: lift from rear. Very high value — flatbed preferred.

battery.location: FRUNK (front storage compartment) — NOT at the rear engine
battery.warnings: Battery in frunk. High-value vehicle — flatbed preferred.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Frunk, Not at Rear Engine
knownIssues.detail: The NSX is mid-engine. The battery is in the front storage compartment (frunk), not at the rear engine. Many operators open the rear engine lid and find no battery — open the FRONT hood.
